meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Template to Escape Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build your MVP in hours, not weeks. Founder-focused guide.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. The Antigravity project template provides a production-ready boilerplate, while Claude Code automates the implementation. By combining these, you bypass boilerplate fatigue and focus exclusively on core business logic, allowing you to validate your SaaS idea and generate revenue faster.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks watching YouTube videos, meticulously typing out code that works in the browser but fails in the real world. You are trapped in the 'Tutorial Loop'—an endless cycle of passive consumption that yields zero revenue and zero product. The market doesn't care about your tutorial progress; it cares about the value you deliver.

Today, we break that cycle. We are pivoting from 'student' to 'founder'. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you gain a battle-tested architecture that handles authentication, database schema, and deployment out of the box. Combined with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you will stop learning frameworks and start building your business.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Clone the base template to establish a secure, scalable architecture immediately. This prevents the 'blank page' syndrome and sets up your CI/CD pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project using the Antigravity boilerplate with Next.js, PostgreSQL, and Clerk authentication.

steps.title: Define Your Data Schema
steps.description: Move beyond basic CRUD. Define your business entities to ensure your database reflects actual user value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a subscription-based SaaS. Include tables for 'User', 'Workspace', and 'SubscriptionPlan' with proper foreign key relationships.

steps.title: Automate API Route Generation
steps.description: Stop writing boilerplate fetch requests. Use Claude Code to map your database schema to secure API endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate secure Next.js API routes for the User and Workspace models. Implement server-side validation using Zod.

steps.title: Implement SaaS Business Logic
steps.description: Inject your unique value proposition. This is where you write the code that differentiates your product from the competition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer function that calculates user usage metrics based on Workspace activity, respecting subscription limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write service/usageTracker.ts --logic 'calculate monthly quota consumption'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Get your MVP live. The goal is to get user feedback today, not next month.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment script for Vercel, ensuring all environment variables are properly mapped for production.

pro_tips: Never write an interface manually; let Claude Code derive them from your database schema to avoid drift.
Prioritize 'Stripe-first' development; if you can't collect money, you don't have a product, you have a hobby.
Use Antigravity's built-in feature flags to hide incomplete modules so you can ship the MVP while iterating on complex features.
Always run your Claude Code prompts with a 'business-first' context to ensure the code generated is maintainable rather than just functional.
Audit your bundle size at every step; SaaS speed is a feature that directly correlates with user conversion rates.
content.next_steps: Now that your MVP is live, focus on acquiring your first five paying users. Do not add features based on gut feeling; add features based on the feedback from those initial users. Your goal is to reach $1,000 MRR before you refactor a single line of code.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap. Stop waiting for permission to be a founder. The market is waiting for your solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
